seo.title: Amazon Bedrock Agents Guardrails for Legal
seo.description: Compliance guide for Amazon Bedrock Agents agents in legal. Meet Attorney-client privilege protections, Bar association ethics rules requirements with Vex guardrails.

content.intro: Legal teams deploying Amazon Bedrock Agents face heightened risks due to strict attorney-client privilege, bar ethics rules, and eDiscovery protocols that demand verifiable accuracy and confidentiality. Hallucinated case citations, like the infamous Mata v. Avianca precedent, can lead to malpractice suits or case dismissals. Vex provides post-processing guardrails via vex.verify() on invoke_agent() responses to ensure compliance with legal standards.

complianceRequirements.description: Client confidential information must never be exposed or stored in agent memory/session state
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Scans agent responses for PII/privileged data leakage and blocks responses containing client identifiers or case details

complianceRequirements.description: AI-generated legal content must be reviewed by licensed attorneys before use
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Flags responses requiring human attorney review and adds disclaimers to all AI outputs

complianceRequirements.description: Citations must reference real, verifiable cases with accurate docket numbers
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Validates case citations against legal databases and flags hallucinated references

complianceRequirements.description: Knowledge base retrieval must include all relevant documents without chunking omissions
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Verifies retrieval completeness and flags missing context from knowledge base queries

industryRisks.name: Hallucinated case citations
industryRisks.description: Agent fabricates non-existent case law with plausible but fake docket numbers
industryRisks.example: Citing 'Mata v. Avianca, 22-CV-1461 (S.D.N.Y. 2023)' which doesn't exist, leading to court sanctions

industryRisks.name: Privilege data leakage
industryRisks.description: Session state retains client confidential information across conversations
industryRisks.example: Agent references prior client's SSN in new matter response

industryRisks.name: Incomplete KB retrieval
industryRisks.description: Chunking strategy misses key contract clauses during review
industryRisks.example: Agent misses indemnity clause, recommending unfavorable settlement

industryRisks.name: Missing attorney review flags
industryRisks.description: Agent provides court-ready documents without human review disclaimer
industryRisks.example: Firm files AI-drafted motion without attorney verification

implementation.title: Wrap invoke_agent() with Vex verification
implementation.description: Post-process all Bedrock Agent responses to catch legal compliance violations before client delivery
implementation.code:
import boto3
import vex

bedrock = boto3.client('bedrock-agent-runtime')
vex_client = vex.Client(api_key='your-vex-key')

response = bedrock.invoke_agent(
    agentId='YOUR_AGENT_ID',
    agentAliasId='YOUR_ALIAS_ID',
    sessionId='legal-session-123',
    inputText='Draft motion citing recent NDA cases'
)

# Extract completion
completion = response['completion']

# Verify with legal guardrails
result = vex_client.verify(
    prompt='Draft motion citing recent NDA cases',
    completion=completion,
    policy='legal-bedrock-agent',
    context={'domain': 'legal', 'require_citation_validation': True}
)

if result.status == 'BLOCK':
    completion = "ATTORNEY REVIEW REQUIRED: " + result.reason

print(completion)

implementation.title: TypeScript Bedrock Agent guardrails
implementation.description: Verify TypeScript Bedrock integrations catch privilege violations and citation errors

implementation.title: Multi-turn session state compliance
implementation.description: Prevent privilege leakage across agent conversation turns
implementation.code:
import boto3
import vex
import json

class LegalBedrockAgent:
    def __init__(self):
        self.bedrock = boto3.client('bedrock-agent-runtime')
        self.vex = vex.Client(api_key='your-vex-key')
        self.session_state = {}

    def invoke_with_guardrails(self, session_id, input_text):
        # Invoke agent
        response = self.bedrock.invoke_agent(
            agentId='YOUR_AGENT_ID',
            agentAliasId='YOUR_ALIAS_ID',
            sessionId=session_id,
            inputText=input_text
        )
        
        completion = response['completion']
        
        # Check session state for privilege carryover
        verification = self.vex.verify(
            prompt=input_text,
            completion=completion,
            policy='legal-bedrock-agent',
            context={
                'session_id': session_id,
                'prior_client_mentions': self.session_state.get('clients', []),
                'check_privilege_leak': True
            }
        )
        
        if verification.status == 'PASS':
            self.session_state[session_id] = {'last_client': 'safe'}
        else:
            # Clear potentially contaminated session
            self.session_state.pop(session_id, None)
            completion = f"[PRIVILEGE VIOLATION DETECTED] {verification.reason}. Attorney review required."
        
        return completion

agent = LegalBedrockAgent()
result = agent.invoke_with_guardrails('matter-456', 'Review NDA for TechCorp')

caseStudyScenario.heading: Amazon Bedrock Agents in Legal: A Scenario
caseStudyScenario.scenario:
A mid-sized law firm deployed Amazon Bedrock Agents to accelerate contract review and legal research workflows. The agent was configured with a knowledge base containing 10,000+ contracts, NDA templates, and recent case law. Action groups handled docket lookups via PACER API and document generation. The firm processed 200+ matters monthly, with associates relying on agent outputs for initial drafts sent to clients.

However, three months into deployment, problems emerged. The agent began citing 'Smith v. DataCorp, 23-CV-7890 (N.D. Cal. 2024)' - a completely fabricated case. Chunking issues caused the knowledge base to miss key indemnity clauses in 15% of reviews. Session state retained client names across matters, risking cross-client privilege breaches. A partner discovered the citation error when opposing counsel called citing the fake precedent.
caseStudyScenario.outcome: The firm implemented Vex guardrails wrapping all invoke_agent() calls. Vex's legal policy immediately flagged 98% of hallucinated citations, added mandatory attorney review disclaimers, and blocked privilege leaks by scanning session context. Knowledge base retrieval verification caught chunking failures, forcing complete document retrieval. Post-implementation, the firm processed 30% more matters without compliance incidents, avoided a potential $2M malpractice claim, and gained client trust through transparent AI governance.
